briefings.headline: Regulators Publish Updated List of Distressed and Underserved Rural Middle-Income Areas
briefings.body: Federal agencies have released an updated list identifying distressed or underserved nonmetropolitan middle-income geographies. The designation is used under Community Reinvestment Act guidelines to determine where certain bank lending and investment activities may receive favorable regulatory consideration, directing financial resources toward rural and small-town communities that face economic hardship or limited access to capital.

sources.title: Agencies release list of distressed or underserved nonmetropolitan middle-income geographies
The Sewer Knows What You Ate

briefings.why_it_matters: Residents and businesses in newly listed communities could find it easier to access bank loans and investment capital, as lenders have a regulatory incentive to direct financing toward these areas.

briefings.headline: Federal Agencies Issue Joint Statement on Protecting Sensitive Data During Bank Examinations
briefings.body: Federal banking agencies have released a joint statement addressing how highly sensitive information should be handled during bank examinations. The guidance aims to establish clearer standards for managing confidential data collected in the examination process, reinforcing expectations for both agencies and the institutions they oversee to safeguard sensitive financial and personal information.

sources.title: Agencies issue joint statement on handling of highly sensitive information during bank examinations
Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former chief lending officer of Heritage State Bank

briefings.why_it_matters: Stronger data-handling standards during bank examinations could reduce the risk that sensitive customer and institutional financial information is mishandled or exposed.

briefings.headline: Trump Administration Set to Announce Nuclear Fuel Deal With Saudi Arabia
briefings.body: The Trump administration is expected to announce a deal that could allow Saudi Arabia to enrich nuclear fuel. U.S. lawmakers and Israeli officials have voiced opposition to the plan, warning that a civilian nuclear project could give the kingdom a pathway to develop nuclear weapons. The announcement is scheduled for Wednesday.

sources.title: Trump Administration to Announce Deal That Could Let Saudis Enrich Nuclear Fuel

briefings.why_it_matters: If Saudi Arabia gains uranium enrichment capabilities, it could shift the nuclear balance in the Middle East and pressure other nations in the region to pursue similar programs.

briefings.headline: Contagious Cancer Discovered in Fish for the First Time
briefings.body: Scientists have identified a transmissible tumor spreading among fish in a lake, marking the first known case of contagious cancer in fish. Previously, such cancers had only been documented in a small number of other animal species. The discovery raises questions about how widely transmissible cancers could exist in nature and prompts researchers to examine whether similar mechanisms could ever pose risks in other species, including humans.

sources.title: Deep in This Lake, a Contagious Cancer Is Spreading

briefings.why_it_matters: Understanding how cancers can spread between animals could reshape what scientists thought were firm boundaries around infectious disease and cancer research, with potential implications for human health monitoring.

briefings.headline: Political appointee review of federal grants could reshape education research priorities
briefings.body: A proposed 100-page overhaul of federal grant rules would add a new review layer by political appointees, raising concerns among education research organizations. Researchers warn the change could hinder the kind of long-term, multidisciplinary work that produced the science of reading movement, which drew on decades of federally supported studies across cognitive science, neuroscience, psychology, linguistics, and classroom research.

sources.title: Education researchers warn political review of federal grants could reshape what gets studied

briefings.why_it_matters: If political appointees gain influence over which studies receive federal funding, the evidence base schools rely on to adopt proven teaching methods could narrow or shift away from peer-driven scientific consensus.

briefings.headline: TreeSize shifts to subscription model, cutting off perpetual-license support renewals
briefings.body: TreeSize, a disk-space analysis tool, is ending support renewals for perpetual-license holders unless they switch to a subscription plan, citing current economic conditions as the driver. Users who purchased a one-time license and want continued support will need to pay recurring fees going forward. The change reflects a broader industry trend of software vendors moving away from traditional perpetual licensing.

sources.title: TreeSize won't renew perpetual-license support unless users subscribe

briefings.why_it_matters: Customers who paid once expecting lifetime ownership could face unexpected recurring costs or lose access to support and updates, affecting how they budget for and rely on the software.

quick_hits.text: Rising temperatures are accelerating the spread of food-borne pathogens like Cyclospora, exposing critical gaps in the U.S. food safety detection system.
The Federal Reserve's annual stress test confirms all large U.S. banks can withstand a severe recession while continuing to lend to households and businesses.

source.title: Federal Reserve Board's annual bank stress test confirms that large banks are well positioned to weather a severe recession and able to continue to lend to households and businesses

quick_hits.text: Ronald Fischer, a fugitive for 20 years, was arrested in New York after hiding under the alias Richard Graydon as a biotech executive.

source.title: On the run for 20 years, most-wanted fugitive caught hiding as a biotech exec

quick_hits.text: Monkeys share geometric shape intuition with humans, challenging the long-held belief that this spatial reasoning ability is uniquely human.

source.title: Monkeys Understand Shapes in Almost the Same Way as Humans

quick_hits.text: Steam Deck sales have cratered by 80 percent following Valve's recent price hike, according to bestseller chart analysis.

source.title: Sales of Valve's Steam Deck crater after recent price hike

voice_blocks.title: This Day
voice_blocks.text: 2019: Chandrayaan-2, the second lunar exploration mission developed by Indian Space Research Organisation after Chandrayaan-1 is launched from Satish Dhawan Space Centre in a GSLV Mark III M1. It consists of a lunar orbiter, and also included the Vikram lander, and the Pragyan lunar rover.

closer.text: "Let the people know the facts, and the country will be safe."
